entries.title: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
entries.snippet: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

entries.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, after Merz criticises war
entries.snippet: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

entries.title: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
entries.snippet: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.

entries.title: Police say they believe abducted child was murdered as body found in Outback
entries.snippet: The five-year-old girl was last seen on Saturday at an Aboriginal town camp near Alice Springs.

entries.title: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
entries.snippet: The governor of Sinaloa, Rubén Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.

entries.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
entries.snippet: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

entries.title: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
entries.snippet: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.

entries.title: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
entries.snippet: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

entries.title: 'I did not expect it': Kenya's Sabastian Sawe welcomed home with jubilant celebrations
entries.snippet: The first man to run a marathon in under two hours in a competitive race received a hero's welcome from supporters and family when he arrived home.

entries.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
entries.snippet: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

entries.title: Christchurch mass killer loses bid to overturn conviction
entries.snippet: White supremacist Brenton Tarrant's appeal is "utterly devoid of merit", a New Zealand court has ruled.

entries.title: King and Queen lay flowers at 9/11 Memorial in New York
entries.snippet: King Charles and Queen Camilla on their state visit to the US visit the memorial in New York.

entries.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

entries.title: Florida lawmakers approve new voting maps to favour Republicans
entries.snippet: The state is the latest to act in a national redistricting war that could shape what political party controls the US House of Representatives.

entries.title: Seven lawsuits filed against OpenAI by families of Canada mass-shooting victims
entries.snippet: The lawsuits, filed in California, accuse OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and abetting a mass shooting by failing to flag the suspect's ChatGPT activity.

entries.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
entries.snippet: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

entries.title: Stranded whale ferried out of German waters in barge
entries.snippet: The final operation to save the whale is being closely followed, after the failure of earlier attempts.

entries.title: Madagascar detains French national over alleged plot to stir unrest
entries.snippet: Malagasy prosecuters used a Whatsapp group as evidence for the detained individuals alleged crimes.

entries.title: The 'Polar Bear Capital' with Arctic gateway ambitions
entries.snippet: Canada is weighing whether a year-round northern trade route to Europe from Churchill, Manitoba, is viable, as climate change speeds Arctic warming.

entries.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
entries.snippet: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

entries.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
entries.snippet: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

entries.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make 'poop talk' OK
entries.snippet: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.

entries.title: The Iraq vet redefining mindfulness, one bourbon at a time
entries.snippet: Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."

entries.title: These fans are boycotting the World Cup. Will they make it a bust?
entries.snippet: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are unhappy with World Cup ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they're deciding not to come, raising concerns across the travel industry.

entries.title: After lackluster tour turnout, Turning Point wraps things up with excited crowd in Idaho
entries.snippet: The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.

entries.title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
entries.snippet: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.

entries.title: Mirroring Gaza, Israel is destroying towns and villages in southern Lebanon
entries.snippet: In southern Lebanon, towns near the border with Israel have been largely destroyed by Israeli demolitions and strikes. Israel says it has been attacking Hezbollah infrastructure, but civilian infrastructure has also been significantly affected.

entries.title: The U.S. economy shows resilience despite the war with Iran
entries.snippet: The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.

entries.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
entries.snippet: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.

entries.title: Taxpayers might pay millions more to fight wildfires as jet fuel skyrockets
entries.snippet: The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.

entries.title: White House says funds to pay TSA and other Homeland Security workers will 'soon run out'
entries.snippet: In a memo late Tuesday to lawmakers, the Office of Management and Budget said money that President Donald Trump tapped to pay Transportation Security Administration and other workers through executive actions will be exhausted by May

entries.title: Florida legislature approves new congressional map intended to boost Republicans in midterms
entries.snippet: The vote came just two days after Gov. Ron DeSantis unveiled his proposal and the same day that the U.S. Supreme Court rolled back a key provision of the Voting Rights Act.

entries.title: Elon Musk tells his side of OpenAI's beginnings in trial pitting him against CEO Sam Altman
entries.snippet: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day Wednesday in the landmark trial that pits the world's richest person against Sam Altman, a fellow OpenAI co-founder he accuses of betraying promises to keep the company as a nonprofit dedicated to humanity's benefit.

entries.title: How the Supreme Court's Louisiana districting decision weakens the Voting Rights Act
entries.snippet: In a 6-3 ruling Wednesday, the U.S. Supreme Court's conservative majority struck down one of Louisiana's majority-Black congressional districts. The decision weakens key protections under the Voting Rights Act and could open the door to broader legal challenges over majority-Blac

entries.title: Hegseth's contentious hearing in Congress reveals partisan divide over Iran war
entries.snippet: For the first time since the U.S. went to war with Iran, Defense Secretary Hegseth faced sharp questions on Wednesday from Congress. During the hearing, the Pentagon revealed that the war so far has cost $25 billion. The fighting is on hold, but the military maintains its blockad

entries.title: News Wrap: Comey denies wrongdoing as he appears in court over new indictment
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Wednesday, Comey appeared in court on charges that he threatened Trump online, Musk took the stand for a second day in his lawsuit against OpenAI, cleanup is underway in Texas after intense storms, prosecutors released new details about the suspect charged with a

entries.title: Why Congress is at an impasse over DHS funding and other critical bills
entries.snippet: After weeks of internal clashes, House Republicans are struggling to move forward on four major pieces of legislation. Those include extending U.S. surveillance authorities, ending the Department of Homeland Security shutdown and advancing key farm policy — all while managing dee

entries.title: Powell says he will stay on Fed board after chair term ends, addressing Trump's attacks
entries.snippet: The Federal Reserve is transitioning to new leadership. Kevin Warsh, Trump's pick for Fed chair, was confirmed by a Senate committee today, clearing the way for a full Senate vote. Jerome Powell said he'll step aside when his term as chair ends in May, but will remain on the boar

entries.title: How upcoming tariff refunds may affect U.S. businesses and consumers
entries.snippet: One of the many uncertainties clouding the U.S. economic picture is tariffs — both the prospect of new ones and upcoming refunds from those struck down by the Supreme Court. Stephanie Sy speaks with Jay Foreman, CEO of the toy maker Basic Fun!, for more on his company's efforts t

entries.title: The local communities hoping America's 250th birthday will help bridge divides
entries.snippet: This summer, many of the celebrations for America's 250th birthday will be grand in scope. But in communities across the country, smaller celebrations are also taking place, hoping to use some of the year's patriotic energy to engage neighbors and transcend political divisions th

entries.title: What it takes to create the perfect pitch for the World Cup
entries.snippet: As the FIFA World Cup approaches this summer, what does it take to create the perfect pitch — or field — for soccer's biggest stage? Economics correspondent Paul Solman heads to Tennessee and puts his body on the line to find out.

entries.title: WATCH: Blanche, newly confirmed ATF head Cekada propose gun regulation rollbacks
entries.snippet: Justice Department officials moved Wednesday to roll back and modify a slate of gun regulations in a dramatic shift in firearm policy pushed by Second Amendment supporters in President Donald Trump's base.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: House approves extension of divisive U.S. spy powers
entries.snippet: The Republican-controlled House approved a three-year reauthorization of a divisive U.S. surveillance program ahead of its expiration on Friday, adding new oversight measures but stopping short of the warrant requirement that critics have demanded.

entries.title: PHOTOS: King Charles and Queen Camilla honor victims at the 9/11 Memorial
entries.snippet: Charles laid flowers at the National 9/11 Memorial and the royal couple spoke with victims' relatives, first responders and local dignitaries before traveling to other events midway through a four-day diplomatic trip to the U.S. to mark 250 years of American independence.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump talks about Putin call, Comey indictment as he welcomes Artemis II astronauts to the White House
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Russia's Vladimir Putin offered to help the U.S. handle Iran's enriched uranium as part of a potential nuclear deal.

entries.title: WATCH: EPA's Zeldin testifies on Trump's budget request in Senate Environment hearing
entries.snippet: Senate Democrats accused the Trump administration of abandoning the Environmental Protection Agency's mission to protect human health and the environment at a congressional hearing Wednesday, slamming agency leadership over a proposal to cut its budget in half.

entries.title: Comey appears in court in Trump threat case that's likely to pose a challenge for Justice Department
entries.snippet: James Comey was indicted in North Carolina on Tuesday on charges of making threats against President Donald Trump related to a photograph he posted on social media last year of seashells arranged in the numbers "86 47." The Justice Department contends those numbers amounted to a

entries.title: WATCH: Hegseth calls congressional Democrats, some Republicans 'biggest adversary' in Iran war
entries.snippet: "The biggest adversary we face at this point are the reckless, feckless and defeatist words of congressional Democrats and some Republicans," Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in his opening remarks in Wednesday's budget hearing before the House Armed Services Committee.

entries.title: Supreme Court sides with anti-abortion center raising 1st Amendment fears about state investigation
entries.snippet: The high court's unanimous ruling is a procedural victory for First Choice Women's Resource Centers, which is challenging a New Jersey investigation of its practices.

entries.title: Supreme Court weakens landmark Civil Rights-era Voting Rights Act, aiding GOP efforts to control the House
entries.snippet: The effect of the ruling may be felt more strongly in 2028 because most filing deadlines for this year's congressional races have passed. Louisiana, though, may have to change its redistricting plan to comply with the decision.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Belgium seeks nationalization of nuclear power plants
entries.snippet: Belgium is reversing its decadeslong phasing-out course, seeking more energy independence by reviving its nuclear plants.

entries.title: First dots on the roadmap to exiting fossil fuels
entries.snippet: The message from inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels was clear. It's not if, but when and how.

entries.title: Will EU bring in a windfall tax on oil companies?
entries.snippet: Five countries, including Germany, want the EU to bring in a so-called windfall tax to target energy companies benefiting from the price crisis. It can be done, but it's complex.

entries.title: Iran war: Pezeshkian says US blockade 'doomed to fail,' Centcom admiral argues otherwise
entries.snippet: Iran's president has said the US naval blockade both violates international law and will not succeed. The US military, meanwhile, is hailing its efficacy impeding oil shipments. Crude prices remain volatile. DW has more.

entries.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW
entries.snippet: Before being imprisoned for 20 years, Jimmy Lai founded Hong Kong's largest pro-democracy newspaper. His son said the award shows that those who fight for the freedom of others "are never alone."

entries.title: Germany news: Economy shows surprise growth despite Iran war
entries.snippet: Germany's economy has grown more than expected, against a backdrop of rising energy costs and uncertain export markets. Meanwhile, VW reports a sharp drop in profits, as pressures mount on the carmaker. DW has more.

entries.title: India news: Exit polls out in key state elections
entries.snippet: Projections suggest mixed outcomes in West Bengal, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry and Tamil Nadu. Narendra Modi's BJP is trying to make inroads in three states where it has never held power. Follow DW.

entries.title: Israel stops Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters
entries.snippet: More than 20 ships were intercepted in a mission aimed at challenging the blockade of the Gaza Strip. Israel dismissed those on board as "attention-seeking agitators."

entries.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
entries.snippet: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

entries.title: Women journalists in Ghana encounter – and overcome – gender barriers
entries.snippet: In Ghana, DW Akademie works with women journalists as they try to advance in their careers despite gender barriers.

entries.title: North Korea faces food shortages as drought threatens crops
entries.snippet: The country's state-run news has said much of the country has been hit by an unusually severe drought. The largely isolated country, with a population of over 26 million, was already facing food shortages.

entries.title: World press freedom hits new low as authoritarianism rises
entries.snippet: With three in four countries "problematic" or worse, the 2026 World Press Freedom Index offers a bleak picture for global media. The conditions for press freedom are rated "satisfactory" in only a few dozen countries.

entries.title: Ecuador starts fracking in Amazon rainforest
entries.snippet: Ecuador is pushing to increase its production of crude oil but faces a backlash from environmentalists and Indigenous groups.

entries.title: US charges Mexico’s Sinaloa state governor with drug trafficking
entries.snippet: The US indictment alleges that Ruben Rocha Moya conspired to move drugs from the Sinaloa Cartel into the US, in exchange for political support

entries.title: Whale 'Timmy' reaches Denmark in rescue effort — in pictures
entries.snippet: A barge carrying a humpback whale stranded in Germany for weeks has reached Danish waters on its journey to the North Sea.

entries.title: Trump threatens US troop cuts in Germany
entries.snippet: Donald Trump says the US is considering a "possible reduction" of its forces in Germany just days after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being "humiliated" by Iran's leadership.

entries.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
entries.snippet: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

entries.title: US top court mulls scrapping Syrians', Haitians' protection
entries.snippet: The Trump administration is appealing lower court orders that blocked it from immediately ending temporary protected status for people from Haiti and Syria.

entries.title: What are the sticking points for the EU's new budget?
entries.snippet: The battle over the next long-term EU budget has begun. The EU's institutions have staked out their positions and are seeking significantly more funding, but it is the 27 member states that will ultimately decide.
